name: amplify
description: Amplify enables developers to develop and deploy cloud-powered mobile and web apps. Amplify Hosting provides a continuous delivery and hosting service for web applications. For more information, see the Amplify Hosting User Guide. The Amplify Framework is a comprehensive set of SDKs, libraries, tools, and documentation for client app development. For more information, see the Amplify Framework

subcommands.name: create-app
subcommands.description: Creates a new Amplify app

options.name: --name
options.description: The name of the Amplify app

options.name: --description
options.description: The description of the Amplify app

options.name: --repository
options.description: The Git repository for the Amplify app

options.name: --platform
options.description: The platform for the Amplify app. For a static app, set the platform type to WEB. For a dynamic server-side rendered (SSR) app, set the platform type to WEB_COMPUTE. For an app requiring Amplify Hosting's original SSR support only, set the platform type to WEB_DYNAMIC. If you are deploying an SSG only app with Next.js version 14 or later, you must set the platform type to WEB_COMPUTE and set the artifacts baseDirectory to .next in the application's build settings. For an example of the build specification settings, see Amplify build settings for a Next.js 14 SSG application in the Amplify Hosting User Guide

options.name: --iam-service-role-arn
options.description: The AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) service role for an Amplify app

options.name: --oauth-token
options.description: The OAuth token for a third-party source control system for an Amplify app. The OAuth token is used to create a webhook and a read-only deploy key using SSH cloning. The OAuth token is not stored. Use oauthToken for repository providers other than GitHub, such as Bitbucket or CodeCommit. To authorize access to GitHub as your repository provider, use accessToken. You must specify either oauthToken or accessToken when you create a new app. Existing Amplify apps deployed from a GitHub repository using OAuth continue to work with CI/CD. However, we strongly recommend that you migrate these apps to use the GitHub App. For more information, see Migrating an existing OAuth app to the Amplify GitHub App in the Amplify User Guide

options.name: --access-token
options.description: The personal access token for a GitHub repository for an Amplify app. The personal access token is used to authorize access to a GitHub repository using the Amplify GitHub App. The token is not stored. Use accessToken for GitHub repositories only. To authorize access to a repository provider such as Bitbucket or CodeCommit, use oauthToken. You must specify either accessToken or oauthToken when you create a new app. Existing Amplify apps deployed from a GitHub repository using OAuth continue to work with CI/CD. However, we strongly recommend that you migrate these apps to use the GitHub App. For more information, see Migrating an existing OAuth app to the Amplify GitHub App in the Amplify User Guide

options.name: --environment-variables
options.description: The environment variables map for an Amplify app.  For a list of the environment variables that are accessible to Amplify by default, see Amplify Environment variables in the Amplify Hosting User Guide

options.name: --enable-branch-auto-build
options.description: Enables the auto building of branches for an Amplify app

options.name: --no-enable-branch-auto-build
options.description: Enables the auto building of branches for an Amplify app

options.name: --enable-branch-auto-deletion
options.description: Automatically disconnects a branch in the Amplify console when you delete a branch from your Git repository

options.name: --no-enable-branch-auto-deletion
options.description: Automatically disconnects a branch in the Amplify console when you delete a branch from your Git repository

options.name: --enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for an Amplify app. This will apply to all branches that are part of this app

options.name: --no-enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for an Amplify app. This will apply to all branches that are part of this app

options.name: --basic-auth-credentials
options.description: The credentials for basic authorization for an Amplify app. You must base64-encode the authorization credentials and provide them in the format user:password

options.name: --custom-rules
options.description: The custom rewrite and redirect rules for an Amplify app

options.name: --tags
options.description: The tag for an Amplify app

options.name: --build-spec
options.description: The build specification (build spec) for an Amplify app

options.name: --custom-headers
options.description: The custom HTTP headers for an Amplify app

options.name: --enable-auto-branch-creation
options.description: Enables automated branch creation for an Amplify app

options.name: --no-enable-auto-branch-creation
options.description: Enables automated branch creation for an Amplify app

options.name: --auto-branch-creation-patterns
options.description: The automated branch creation glob patterns for an Amplify app

options.name: --auto-branch-creation-config
options.description: The automated branch creation configuration for an Amplify app

options.name: --cache-config
options.description: The cache configuration for the Amplify app

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

options.name: --generate-cli-skeleton
options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command

subcommands.name: create-backend-environment
subcommands.description: Creates a new backend environment for an Amplify app.  This API is available only to Amplify Gen 1 applications where the backend is created using Amplify Studio or the Amplify command line interface (CLI). This API isn’t available to Amplify Gen 2 applications. When you deploy an application with Amplify Gen 2, you provision the app's backend infrastructure using Typescript code

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --environment-name
options.description: The name for the backend environment

options.name: --stack-name
options.description: The AWS CloudFormation stack name of a backend environment

options.name: --deployment-artifacts
options.description: The name of deployment artifacts

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

options.name: --generate-cli-skeleton
options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command

subcommands.name: create-branch
subcommands.description: Creates a new branch for an Amplify app

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name for the branch

options.name: --description
options.description: The description for the branch

options.name: --stage
options.description: Describes the current stage for the branch

options.name: --framework
options.description: The framework for the branch

options.name: --enable-notification
options.description: Enables notifications for the branch

options.name: --no-enable-notification
options.description: Enables notifications for the branch

options.name: --enable-auto-build
options.description: Enables auto building for the branch

options.name: --no-enable-auto-build
options.description: Enables auto building for the branch

options.name: --environment-variables
options.description: The environment variables for the branch

options.name: --basic-auth-credentials
options.description: The basic authorization credentials for the branch. You must base64-encode the authorization credentials and provide them in the format user:password

options.name: --enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for the branch

options.name: --no-enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for the branch

options.name: --enable-performance-mode
options.description: Enables performance mode for the branch. Performance mode optimizes for faster hosting performance by keeping content cached at the edge for a longer interval. When performance mode is enabled, hosting configuration or code changes can take up to 10 minutes to roll out

options.name: --no-enable-performance-mode
options.description: Enables performance mode for the branch. Performance mode optimizes for faster hosting performance by keeping content cached at the edge for a longer interval. When performance mode is enabled, hosting configuration or code changes can take up to 10 minutes to roll out

options.name: --tags
options.description: The tag for the branch

options.name: --build-spec
options.description: The build specification (build spec) for the branch

options.name: --ttl
options.description: The content Time To Live (TTL) for the website in seconds

options.name: --display-name
options.description: The display name for a branch. This is used as the default domain prefix

options.name: --enable-pull-request-preview
options.description: Enables pull request previews for this branch

options.name: --no-enable-pull-request-preview
options.description: Enables pull request previews for this branch

options.name: --pull-request-environment-name
options.description: The Amplify environment name for the pull request

options.name: --backend-environment-arn
options.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for a backend environment that is part of a Gen 1 Amplify app.  This field is available to Amplify Gen 1 apps only where the backend is created using Amplify Studio or the Amplify command line interface (CLI)

options.name: --backend
options.description: The backend for a Branch of an Amplify app. Use for a backend created from an CloudFormation stack. This field is available to Amplify Gen 2 apps only. When you deploy an application with Amplify Gen 2, you provision the app's backend infrastructure using Typescript code

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

options.name: --generate-cli-skeleton
options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command

subcommands.name: create-deployment
subcommands.description: Creates a deployment for a manually deployed Amplify app. Manually deployed apps are not connected to a Git repository.  The maximum duration between the CreateDeployment call and the StartDeployment call cannot exceed 8 hours. If the duration exceeds 8 hours, the StartDeployment call and the associated Job will fail

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name of the branch to use for the job

options.name: --file-map
options.description: An optional file map that contains the file name as the key and the file content md5 hash as the value. If this argument is provided, the service will generate a unique upload URL per file. Otherwise, the service will only generate a single upload URL for the zipped files

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. options.name: --auto-sub-domain-creation-patterns

options.name: --auto-sub-domain-iam-role

options.name: --certificate-settings
options.description: The type of SSL/TLS certificate to use for your custom domain. If you don't specify a certificate type, Amplify uses the default certificate that it provisions and manages for you

options.name: --starting-token
options.description:
A token to specify where to start paginating.  This is the
NextToken from a previously truncated response.
For usage examples, see Pagination in the AWS Command Line Interface User
Guide

options.name: --page-size
options.description:
The size of each page to get in the AWS service call.  This
does not affect the number of items returned in the command's
output.  Setting a smaller page size results in more calls to
the AWS service, retrieving fewer items in each call.  This can
help prevent the AWS service calls from timing out.
For usage examples, see Pagination in the AWS Command Line Interface User
Guide

options.name: --max-items
options.description:
The total number of items to return in the command's output.
If the total number of items available is more than the value
specified, a NextToken is provided in the command's
output.  To resume pagination, provide the
NextToken value in the starting-token
argument of a subsequent command.  Do not use the
NextToken response element directly outside of the
AWS CLI.
For usage examples, see Pagination in the AWS Command Line Interface User
Guide

options.name: --generate-cli-skeleton
options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command

subcommands.name: list-artifacts
subcommands.description: Returns a list of artifacts for a specified app, branch, and job

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name of a branch that is part of an Amplify app

options.name: --job-id
options.description: The unique ID for a job

options.name: --next-token
options.description: A pagination token. Set to null to start listing artifacts from start. If a non-null pagination token is returned in a result, pass its value in here to list more artifacts

options.name: --max-results
options.description: The maximum number of records to list in a single response

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

options.name: --next-token

options.name: --max-results
options.description: The maximum number of records to list in a single response

subcommands.name: start-deployment
subcommands.description: Starts a deployment for a manually deployed app. Manually deployed apps are not connected to a Git repository.  The maximum duration between the CreateDeployment call and the StartDeployment call cannot exceed 8 hours. If the duration exceeds 8 hours, the StartDeployment call and the associated Job will fail

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name of the branch to use for the deployment job

options.name: --job-id
options.description: The job ID for this deployment that is generated by the CreateDeployment request

options.name: --source-url
options.description: The source URL for the deployment that is used when calling StartDeployment without CreateDeployment. The source URL can be either an HTTP GET URL that is publicly accessible and downloads a single .zip file, or an Amazon S3 bucket and prefix

options.name: --source-url-type
options.description: The type of source specified by the sourceURL. If the value is ZIP, the source is a .zip file. If the value is BUCKET_PREFIX, the source is an Amazon S3 bucket and prefix. If no value is specified, the default is ZIP

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

subcommands.name: start-job
subcommands.description: Starts a new job for a branch of an Amplify app

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name of the branch to use for the job

options.name: --job-id
options.description: The unique ID for an existing job. This is required if the value of jobType is RETRY

options.name: --job-type
options.description: Describes the type for the job. The job type RELEASE starts a new job with the latest change from the specified branch. This value is available only for apps that are connected to a repository.  The job type RETRY retries an existing job. If the job type value is RETRY, the jobId is also required

options.name: --job-reason
options.description: A descriptive reason for starting the job

options.name: --commit-id
options.description: The commit ID from a third-party repository provider for the job

options.name: --commit-message
options.description: The commit message from a third-party repository provider for the job

options.name: --commit-time
options.description: The commit date and time for the job

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. subcommands.name: update-app
subcommands.description: Updates an existing Amplify app

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --name
options.description: The name for an Amplify app

options.name: --description
options.description: The description for an Amplify app

options.name: --platform
options.description: The platform for the Amplify app. For a static app, set the platform type to WEB. For a dynamic server-side rendered (SSR) app, set the platform type to WEB_COMPUTE. For an app requiring Amplify Hosting's original SSR support only, set the platform type to WEB_DYNAMIC. If you are deploying an SSG only app with Next.js version 14 or later, you must set the platform type to WEB_COMPUTE

options.name: --iam-service-role-arn
options.description: The AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) service role for an Amplify app

options.name: --environment-variables
options.description: The environment variables for an Amplify app

options.name: --enable-branch-auto-build
options.description: Enables branch auto-building for an Amplify app

options.name: --no-enable-branch-auto-build
options.description: Enables branch auto-building for an Amplify app

options.name: --enable-branch-auto-deletion
options.description: Automatically disconnects a branch in the Amplify console when you delete a branch from your Git repository

options.name: --no-enable-branch-auto-deletion
options.description: Automatically disconnects a branch in the Amplify console when you delete a branch from your Git repository

options.name: --enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for an Amplify app

options.name: --no-enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for an Amplify app

options.name: --basic-auth-credentials
options.description: The basic authorization credentials for an Amplify app. You must base64-encode the authorization credentials and provide them in the format user:password

options.name: --custom-rules
options.description: The custom redirect and rewrite rules for an Amplify app

options.name: --build-spec
options.description: The build specification (build spec) for an Amplify app

options.name: --custom-headers
options.description: The custom HTTP headers for an Amplify app

options.name: --enable-auto-branch-creation
options.description: Enables automated branch creation for an Amplify app

options.name: --no-enable-auto-branch-creation
options.description: Enables automated branch creation for an Amplify app

options.name: --auto-branch-creation-patterns
options.description: Describes the automated branch creation glob patterns for an Amplify app

options.name: --auto-branch-creation-config
options.description: The automated branch creation configuration for an Amplify app

options.name: --repository
options.description: The name of the Git repository for an Amplify app

options.name: --oauth-token
options.description: The OAuth token for a third-party source control system for an Amplify app. The OAuth token is used to create a webhook and a read-only deploy key using SSH cloning. The OAuth token is not stored. Use oauthToken for repository providers other than GitHub, such as Bitbucket or CodeCommit. To authorize access to GitHub as your repository provider, use accessToken. You must specify either oauthToken or accessToken when you update an app. Existing Amplify apps deployed from a GitHub repository using OAuth continue to work with CI/CD. However, we strongly recommend that you migrate these apps to use the GitHub App. For more information, see Migrating an existing OAuth app to the Amplify GitHub App in the Amplify User Guide

options.name: --access-token
options.description: The personal access token for a GitHub repository for an Amplify app. The personal access token is used to authorize access to a GitHub repository using the Amplify GitHub App. The token is not stored. Use accessToken for GitHub repositories only. To authorize access to a repository provider such as Bitbucket or CodeCommit, use oauthToken. You must specify either accessToken or oauthToken when you update an app. Existing Amplify apps deployed from a GitHub repository using OAuth continue to work with CI/CD. However, we strongly recommend that you migrate these apps to use the GitHub App. For more information, see Migrating an existing OAuth app to the Amplify GitHub App in the Amplify User Guide

options.name: --cache-config
options.description: The cache configuration for the Amplify app

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

options.name: --generate-cli-skeleton
options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command

subcommands.name: update-branch
subcommands.description: Updates a branch for an Amplify app

options.name: --app-id
options.description: The unique ID for an Amplify app

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name of the branch

options.name: --description
options.description: The description for the branch

options.name: --framework
options.description: The framework for the branch

options.name: --stage
options.description: Describes the current stage for the branch

options.name: --enable-notification
options.description: Enables notifications for the branch

options.name: --no-enable-notification
options.description: Enables notifications for the branch

options.name: --enable-auto-build
options.description: Enables auto building for the branch

options.name: --no-enable-auto-build
options.description: Enables auto building for the branch

options.name: --environment-variables
options.description: The environment variables for the branch

options.name: --basic-auth-credentials
options.description: The basic authorization credentials for the branch. You must base64-encode the authorization credentials and provide them in the format user:password

options.name: --enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for the branch

options.name: --no-enable-basic-auth
options.description: Enables basic authorization for the branch

options.name: --enable-performance-mode
options.description: Enables performance mode for the branch. Performance mode optimizes for faster hosting performance by keeping content cached at the edge for a longer interval. When performance mode is enabled, hosting configuration or code changes can take up to 10 minutes to roll out

options.name: --no-enable-performance-mode
options.description: Enables performance mode for the branch. Performance mode optimizes for faster hosting performance by keeping content cached at the edge for a longer interval. When performance mode is enabled, hosting configuration or code changes can take up to 10 minutes to roll out

options.name: --build-spec
options.description: The build specification (build spec) for the branch

options.name: --ttl
options.description: The content Time to Live (TTL) for the website in seconds

options.name: --display-name
options.description: The display name for a branch. This is used as the default domain prefix

options.name: --enable-pull-request-preview
options.description: Enables pull request previews for this branch

options.name: --no-enable-pull-request-preview
options.description: Enables pull request previews for this branch

options.name: --pull-request-environment-name
options.description: The Amplify environment name for the pull request

options.name: --backend-environment-arn
options.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for a backend environment that is part of a Gen 1 Amplify app.  This field is available to Amplify Gen 1 apps only where the backend is created using Amplify Studio or the Amplify command line interface (CLI)

options.name: --backend
options.description: The backend for a Branch of an Amplify app. Use for a backend created from an CloudFormation stack. This field is available to Amplify Gen 2 apps only. When you deploy an application with Amplify Gen 2, you provision the app's backend infrastructure using Typescript code

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command

subcommands.name: update-webhook
subcommands.description: Updates a webhook

options.name: --webhook-id
options.description: The unique ID for a webhook

options.name: --branch-name
options.description: The name for a branch that is part of an Amplify app

options.name: --description
options.description: The description for a webhook

options.name: --cli-input-json
options.description: Performs service operation based on the JSON string provided. The JSON string follows the format provided by ``--generate-cli-skeleton``. If other arguments are provided on the command line, the CLI values will override the JSON-provided values. It is not possible to pass arbitrary binary values using a JSON-provided value as the string will be taken literally

options.name: --generate-cli-skeleton
options.description: Prints a JSON skeleton to standard output without sending an API request. If provided with no value or the value ``input``, prints a sample input JSON that can be used as an argument for ``--cli-input-json``. If provided with the value ``output``, it validates the command inputs and returns a sample output JSON for that command
